title: The Theme of Infinity and its Break in 3
display_title: The Theme of Infinity and its Break in 3
wikitext:
[]: The Theme of Infinity and it's Break in 3             Quote Post by unitM » Fri Jan 10, 2014 5:32 pm Hi guys. I posted part of this theory a long time ago in a much smaller post. Everytime I rewatch the third Rebuild after a short break, though, I keep finding reason to believe that this infinite symbol plays out such a powerful role. We're first explicitly introduced to the theme of infinity when Kaworu mentions the Failures of Infinity when descending to the climax of the movie. We're given an image of a solidified red wall, patterned and layered with Unit 01 look-alike dolls. Kaworu tells Shinji to brush them off at the time until he meets his descent. Evangelion Unit 13 was designed to open up the lock to the Central Dogma, we're told by Kaworu. Just before 13's eyes glow white, Kaworu relaxes Shinji and tells him to synchronize his breathing to Kaworu's. The synchronization is another key to the infinity theme in this movie too. But prior to that, wasn't there another time when Shinji and Kaworu were synchronized? Kaworu says it himself in the same instance of telling Shinji to care to his breathing: when they were playing the piano. When Kaworu and Shinji sat down for their first piano lesson, Kaworu was the one gracefully leading. Shinji soon caught up and suddenly, they were playing in synchronization. The symbol infinity rears it's head again. Shinji wakes up, not 15(which is a significant number in the series) but 14 years later. The movie title itself expresses a theme of infinity and synchronization: You Can (Not) Redo. Shinji wakes up in completely foreign territories, where many of the personalities of people he's known suddenly act paralyzingly cold towards him now. The continuation and, if I may, synchronization is broken from what he fell asleep to vs. what he's experiencing now. Yet there are some peculiar moments with the theme of infinity in this movie too. Despite all the discontinuation Shinji feels, in the background, we, the viewer, are shown that there is a continuation with the characters. We know Misato is a continuation from the 14 year gap because she doesn't execute Shinji. Shinji probably doesn't realize this. The same can be said to Asuka, who is glass-crackingly aggressive towards Shinji. She only expresses a nurturing quality at the end... when Shinji is flopped over as a ragdoll from the previous events. Kaworu brings Shinji to confront this desynchronization when, after Unit 13 opens the Doors of Guf, he comforts Shinji in telling him that the activation was not his fault. Shinji looks at Kaworu and states he has no idea what he's talking about. The explicit desynchronization of the movie happened around the climax. Shinji withdrew the lances from the inanimate Lilith, while Kaworu begged him to stop. A loud alarm signalled this break in synchronization, followed by Kaworu's plug darkening and withdrawing. Kaworu acknowledged this as well. When Kaworu and Shinji first entered Evangelion 13, there was a golden infinite symbol behind them, showing they were in sync. After Shinji pushed forward alone, this symbol is also clearly broken. I'm not sure what to make of it. The first great overt breaking of the synchronization was shown in Unit 13. Throughout the movie, there were punches thrown at Shinji to break his rhythm, but only the most powerfully shown when Shinji nearly called out another impact. Was Gendou responsible for the break? Every 15 years, something bad seems to happen, and yet Shinji was woken up exactly on the 14th year and forced to almost drive the world to extinction again. The most climactic desync also occurs when Shinji acts differently than what everyone wants... except for perhaps Gendou. He seemed awful calm about the whole instance, before and after. Ultimately, I think this theme of reccuring(see what I did there?) is really important.

[]:             Quote Post by The Dragon Mask » Mon Jan 13, 2014 2:22 am Interesting idea, I definitely think it is possible that "infinity" is the goal of instrumentality. If all people are reduced to one being, there are no others to reflect this being's self into, meaning it is eternally alone, and might as well be zero. But, instrumentality is obviously more than that, it's an infinite range of possibilities, if we take into consideration EoTV's talks about such matters. One, zero, and infinity are all one and the same. I probably didn't explain it very well, but I do think the above is true essentially, that instrumentality is very "infinite" in a way, since it is one being, all beings, and theoretically if one being has no other to compare itself to, how can it be considered to exist at all? (Therefore it is zero) Moving on, could you clarify how you jump to "synchronization" being a symbol of infinity? Also, you are saying that the time jump is a break in sync, and that returning to this sync will cause the world to be whole again? What are you saying synchronization or infinity will bring? Do you mean that synchronization is like a synch ratio or like, a common goal among the characters? In the case of 1.0 and 2.0, most characters besides Seele, Gendou/Fuyutsuki, all had the "synchronous goal" of killing the Angels. Is that what you mean? Also, I've realized over time that the "break in sync" just before Shinji pulls out the spears is not because of an automatic mechanism, it is Shinji consciously cutting off Kaworu's controls. I'm not seeing how their is evidence of a cycle of disaster every 15 years. Only twice now.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Mon Jan 13, 2014 4:22 am Well first, Fourth Impact happened fourteen years after N3I, not fifteen like between 2I and N3I, so there don't seem to have some law of cycles here(unless the true apocalypse will come in FINAL, and it happens one years after the events of Q) Now I thing that the theme the OP is looking for is that through mutual understanding and help, the possibilities are infinite(ie nothing is impossible), "synchronization" here designing that mutual help. That ties with Kaworu's words when he was duetting with Shinji that two people together can make great things. The "desynchronization" happens when someones stop trying to understand the others and starts to act for selfish reasons : it's Gendo ready to end the world to be reunited with Yui, Shinji joining the fight against Zeruel only to save Rei at any cost(even though the consequences where unknown to him and unwanted) and in Q where he stops listening to Kaworu and pull out the spears in his mad quest for redemption. As for the time jump being a break in synch, I think that he's referring to how the rest of the cast got to live the past 14 years, learning more about the conspiracy, living new experiences and overall undergoing character development, while Shinji stagnated in his stays inside EVA-01, making him essentially a relic of the past when he came back in the world of the living, a relic completely ignorant of everything the others learned about the big conspiracy to end the world. That already is a big break of synchronization, but to make matters worse, Shinji is help responsible for at least part of the hellish situation the others ended into for the past 14 years, which made all the relationships he built before the time skip extremely strenuous at best, and irremediably destroyed at worst. And I think that one of the themes of FINAL will be Shinji learning to "synchronize" with the world again, meaning to learn to take into account the other's words and wishes, even if they don't correspond to his own wishes(which would also makes him more resistant to Gendo's manipulations), and also to have him starting to try to mend his relationship with everyone he cared for. --- Now for this part : unitM wrote: The most climactic desync also occurs when Shinji acts differently than what everyone wants... except for perhaps Gendou. He seemed awful calm about the whole instance, before and after.

[]:             Quote Post by unitM » Mon Jan 13, 2014 8:39 pm I'm fairly certain that instrumentality represents infinity. Why? Once everyone's in an instrumental state, it, from what we know, lasts forever. Souls are merged. People see each other's deepest, darkest, and most intimate memories. Walls are broken down. For infinity to exist - just think of the symbol - there has to be no "walls" or breaks. It's a sign of repetition. Instrumentality definitely shows aspects of infinity. I can't believe there's not anymore of a discussion about this. Look at the title of the movie. 3. It represents broken infinite, either turned on it's side or simply representing the third(but not repeated) number of the series. You Can (Not) Redo. Umm, hello... A few other important points I came up with today, considering this original post was a little rushed. Some may recall Kaworu mentioning a double-entry system when entering Unit 13. Well, surprise surprise, this is also another theme of repetition. If anyone has taken financial business accounting in the past, you'll recognize the words double-entry system almost immediately. Because the accounting system refers to what is received and what is given , it is referred to as a double-entry system. This can especially be seen on, without going into too much depth, financial reports, such as the balance sheet. The balance sheet, in financial accounting, must always balance. When Shinji and Kaworu entered 13, they were balanced. Yet this balance was thrown off(in the same way that Asuka was thrown off the hill of skulls), at first when Kaworu noticed the two spears. Suddenly, one of the pilots was fighting, while the other was thinking. Of course, this was aside from the fact that there were two of the same spears - this is our first signal of disharmony. Then, a more overt signal became clear: one of the pilots was shut out of the Evangelion control system, while the other one was not. The double-entry system was failing. At least between the two pilots in 13. When 13 was awakened, we can see the disharmony's true colours. One pilot was in tears. The other was calm. One pilot dies. One survives. That is, of course, unless someone else reaped the second part of the "balance." That appears to make sense, considering Kaworu was the one that mentioned the double-entry system, not Gendou. Gendou simply stated that Shinji will pilot with Kaworu but did not mention a double-entry system. But perhaps it was left out by Gendou because he knew he was deceptively reaping the other half of the system anyways and mentioning it presents a risk, however slight, to his plan. How I'm seeing things right now is that instrumentality represents infinity. Kaworu is Seele's child, and Seele's goal was to promote instrumentality. Unsurprisingly, Kaworu had no issue being vague about the descent into Central Dogma to, his knowledge, promote instrumentality. He even told Shinji, in a very calm manner, to ignore the lifeless failures of infinity that lined the walls. Why? Likely because he was confident Shinji would reach infinity and the failures would only serve as a distraction. The first thing that Kaworu really reacts to is the two spears being the same - at this point, he thought something was different with the instrumentality he was sent down to bring about. I don't quite have a grasp of how synchronization represents infinity. Despite all my theorizing, 3 was awfully vague. But there were still some explicit images of synchronization representing infinity. From start to finish, between Shinji and Kaworu: Shinji is reborn into the world. He isn't able to connect with anyone. Suddenly, he's able to synchronize over-dramatically with Kaworu in his piano duet. Shinji and Kaworu enter 13 via a double entry system. Before entering Lillith's lair, Kaworu explicitly asks Shinji to synchronize his breath to his . If you look behind Kaworu and Shinji when they are piloting 13, there is a clear visual image of infinity being expressed(with piano notes forming the symbol). When this synchronization is broken as described above, this symbol is also broken. I think synchronization, taken from this movie, is a separate entity than expressed in the series. Or at least, a branch off of. I'm not sure. But. If reaching infinite is reaching instrumentality, as theorized in this thread, there are many themes that are also shared between instrumentality and infinite from the series. Still, I think this is a seperate instance that occurred in 13, and we really don't know much about 13. However, it is interesting to note that 13 is not Shinji's mom in the Eva sense. Perhaps Gendou required another pilot, whom he knew Shinji would "sync" with, to replace his mom, since Fuyu toppled over Shinji's relationship with Rei and Rei seems pretty brainless in this movie, anyways. Despite Gendou having seemingly-different plans than Seele(and Kaworu, who expected instrumentality and infinite), Kaworu could have been useful to Gendou in starting the impact(because of the sync) and also removing Kaworu from this equation. But this is common knowledge. Another thing I have not fully explored yet: Shinji's stumbling-through-the-halls chaos theme actually sounds repetitive and loop-able. I believe someone here discovered that the choker that Misato outfitted Shinji with had the same chaotic theme embedded into it, if you translate the glyphs. And yet, if the song itself sounds very much like a loop, I wonder if it in essence represents a state of infinite. And with that in mind, I've been stretching my mind a peculiar way: what if Shinji's "infinite" is actually chaos? That would surely tie into End of Evangelion, wouldn't it? Shinji rejected instrumentality, or as we like to call it here, infinite. Why? Because it was too chaotic for him. Then what does Kaworu removing and equipping the choker symbolize? I haven't had time to wash this thought over but it is interesting to think about. Even if my point on there being no evidence behind the year cycles, doesn't it seem a bit... strange? How Shinji was woken up 14 years after N3I and suddenly initiates an impact, but it's not a full impact ? Doesn't it seem odd that, as soon as Shinji woke up, Gendou sent Rei to free him? Doesn't that seem a bit... accelerated? And, unsurprisingly, the impact doesn't complete itself either? Regardless of whether Shinji cuts off Kaworu's controls consciously or not, what's important is that it happens. There is an act of unbalance, be it orchestrated by Gendou or Shinji, prior to Kaworu losing his shit in the first place. The repetition in infinite is lost, and suddenly, Kaworu is probably wishing to himself that he had explained the failures of infinity in the first place(haha). But really. If Shinji consciously cut off Kaworu, and he did not get his desire, then someone else did. So we can say that that someone else orchestrated the mess, despite Shinji consciously cutting off Kaworu. If it was unconscious, well, that explains itself: someone orchestrated it. @ElMariachi I have no objections to what you said but I think the theme of infinite can be extended to instrumentality, despite that connection not being explicitly stated.[/list]

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Tue Jan 14, 2014 5:52 am That Instrumentality is connected to Infinity is pretty much stated in Q, the big red Eva statues, are called "Failures of infinity/those who failed to merge with Infinity " after all. That ties with SEELE's doctrine that Instrumentality will be the apex of mankind's evolution, which make it evolve to a godlike status. But also be cautious, because this Infinity isn't necessarily a good thing, after all SEELE manipulated mankind to bring forth the HIP, and planned to unleash it to the Lilin without their consent. I think that there are actually two visions of "Infinity" in Rebuild : the one as seen by SEELE and that I talked above, and the other stated by Kaworu, where "Infinity"(meaning accomplishing even the impossible) can be reached via "synchronization"(two people or maybe more working together toward a same goal) But that doesn't meant that Kaworu's intention are to thwart SEELE's vision of "Infinity" : as it was debated on other threads, there was a lot of important information that he omitted to Shinji, and he never said that SEELE's HIP was a bad thing for mankind(in contrast with NGE where he stated it), which could be hints that he's actually still loyal to SEELE and intended to trigger Instrumentality at the same time as repairing the world or whatever the hell the Cassius spear was supposed to do. --- unitM wrote: That is, of course, unless someone else reaped the second part of the "balance." That appears to make sense, considering Kaworu was the one that mentioned the double-entry system, not Gendou. Gendou simply stated that Shinji will pilot with Kaworu but did not mention a double-entry system. But perhaps it was left out by Gendou because he knew he was deceptively reaping the other half of the system anyways and mentioning it presents a risk, however slight, to his plan.

[]:             Quote Post by unitM » Thu Jan 23, 2014 10:52 pm ElMariachi wrote: That Instrumentality is connected to Infinity is pretty much stated in Q, the big red Eva statues, are called "Failures of infinity/those who failed to merge with Infinity " after all. That ties with SEELE's doctrine that Instrumentality will be the apex of mankind's evolution, which make it evolve to a godlike status. But also be cautious, because this Infinity isn't necessarily a good thing, after all SEELE manipulated mankind to bring forth the HIP, and planned to unleash it to the Lilin without their consent. I think that there are actually two visions of "Infinity" in Rebuild : the one as seen by SEELE and that I talked above, and the other stated by Kaworu, where "Infinity"(meaning accomplishing even the impossible) can be reached via "synchronization"(two people or maybe more working together toward a same goal) But that doesn't meant that Kaworu's intention are to thwart SEELE's vision of "Infinity" : as it was debated on other threads, there was a lot of important information that he omitted to Shinji, and he never said that SEELE's HIP was a bad thing for mankind(in contrast with NGE where he stated it), which could be hints that he's actually still loyal to SEELE and intended to trigger Instrumentality at the same time as repairing the world or whatever the hell the Cassius spear was supposed to do. --- I think you're overanalyzing here : Gendo didn't mentioned the double-entry to Shinji simply because he didn't needed to know at that moment, Gendo just appeared to show to Shinji that he's still alive and is indeed still in charge of NERV and give him in general terms what he expect Shinji to do, Kaworu would later fill him about the details. There is also a theory that Gendo made the "encounter" so short because he couldn't bear to face his son after everything Gendo did and what he become, and because of what he intends to do to his son very soon.(break him mentally and forces him to fight his former friends)

 about 13. However, it is interesting to note that 13 is not Shinji's mom in the Eva sense. Perhaps Gendou required another pilot, whom he knew Shinji would "sync" with, to replace his mom, since Fuyu toppled over Shinji's relationship with Rei and Rei seems pretty brainless in this movie, anyways. Despite Gendou having seemingly-different plans than Seele(and Kaworu, who expected instrumentality and infinite), Kaworu could have been useful to Gendou in starting the impact(because of the sync) and also removing Kaworu from this equation. But this is common knowledge. Another thing I have not fully explored yet: Shinji's stumbling-through-the-halls chaos theme actually sounds repetitive and loop-able. I believe someone here discovered that the choker that Misato outfitted Shinji with had the same chaotic theme embedded into it, if you translate the glyphs. And yet, if the song itself sounds very much like a loop, I wonder if it in essence represents a state of infinite. And with that in mind, I've been stretching my mind a peculiar way: what if Shinji's "infinite" is actually chaos? That would surely tie into End of Evangelion, wouldn't it? Shinji rejected instrumentality, or as we like to call it here, infinite. Why? Because it was too chaotic for him. Then what does Kaworu removing and equipping the choker symbolize? I haven't had time to wash this thought over but it is interesting to think about. Even if my point on there being no evidence behind the year cycles, doesn't it seem a bit... strange? How Shinji was woken up 14 years after N3I and suddenly initiates an impact, but it's not a full impact ? Doesn't it seem odd that, as soon as Shinji woke up, Gendou sent Rei to free him? Doesn't that seem a bit... accelerated? And, unsurprisingly, the impact doesn't complete itself either? Regardless of whether Shinji cuts off Kaworu's controls consciously or not, what's important is that it happens. There is an act of unbalance, be it orchestrated by Gendou or Shinji, prior to Kaworu losing his shit in the first place. The repetition in infinite is lost, and suddenly, Kaworu is probably wishing to himself that he had explained the failures of infinity in the first place(haha). But really. If Shinji consciously cut

Article: The Theme of Infinity and its Break in 3
 Kaworu losing his shit in the first place. The repetition in infinite is lost, and suddenly, Kaworu is probably wishing to himself that he had explained the failures of infinity in the first place(haha). But really. If Shinji consciously cut off Kaworu, and he did not get his desire, then someone else did. So we can say that that someone else orchestrated the mess, despite Shinji consciously cutting off Kaworu. If it was unconscious, well, that explains itself: someone orchestrated it. @ElMariachi I have no objections to what you said but I think the theme of infinite can be extended to instrumentality, despite that connection not being explicitly stated.[/list]

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Tue Jan 14, 2014 5:52 am That Instrumentality is connected to Infinity is pretty much stated in Q, the big red Eva statues, are called "Failures of infinity/those who failed to merge with Infinity " after all. That ties with SEELE's doctrine that Instrumentality will be the apex of mankind's evolution, which make it evolve to a godlike status. But also be cautious, because this Infinity isn't necessarily a good thing, after all SEELE manipulated mankind to bring forth the HIP, and planned to unleash it to the Lilin without their consent. I think that there are actually two visions of "Infinity" in Rebuild : the one as seen by SEELE and that I talked above, and the other stated by Kaworu, where "Infinity"(meaning accomplishing even the impossible) can be reached via "synchronization"(two people or maybe more working together toward a same goal) But that doesn't meant that Kaworu's intention are to thwart SEELE's vision of "Infinity" : as it was debated on other threads, there was a lot of important information that he omitted to Shinji, and he never said that SEELE's HIP was a bad thing for mankind(in contrast with NGE where he stated it), which could be hints that he's actually still loyal to SEELE and intended to trigger Instrumentality at the same time as repairing the world or whatever the hell the Cassius spear was supposed to do. --- unitM wrote: That is, of course, unless someone else reaped the second part of the "balance." That appears to make sense, considering Kaworu was the one that mentioned the double-entry system, not Gendou. Gendou simply stated that Shinji will pilot with Kaworu

Article: The Theme of Infinity and its Break in 3
 unless someone else reaped the second part of the "balance." That appears to make sense, considering Kaworu was the one that mentioned the double-entry system, not Gendou. Gendou simply stated that Shinji will pilot with Kaworu but did not mention a double-entry system. But perhaps it was left out by Gendou because he knew he was deceptively reaping the other half of the system anyways and mentioning it presents a risk, however slight, to his plan.

 and is indeed still in charge of NERV and give him in general terms what he expect Shinji to do, Kaworu would later fill him about the details. There is also a theory that Gendo made the "encounter" so short because he couldn't bear to face his son after everything Gendo did and what he become, and because of what he intends to do to his son very soon.(break him mentally and forces him to fight his former friends)
